summary: Backend-only regression for CashClick API. 35 of 37 tests passed; 2 skipped (test_withdraw_success and dependent test_withdraw_action_rejected_refunds) because accumulated user balance after random reward tests was below 1000 points (₹10) needed for the minimum withdrawal. All targeted features from the review request work as specified: health/config/banners, full auth flow incl. duplicate rejections, 11 seeded games with chances=10 and interstitial on every 5th play, watch-rewarded chance refill, task list/detail/submit, campaign confirm creates approved campaign withdrawal entry, all Explore flows (checkin streak day 1 = 10 pts, spin/scratch 10-20, visits start→complete with daily lock + reset, watch 30s gap enforcement, surveys 10/done flag, quizzes correct-pattern scoring 7*correct = 70), wallet balance/conversion/transactions filter, insufficient-balance 400, refer/profile, admin auth + protection, dashboard counters, task-submission approve credits user, config & games-config patches reflect immediately, and CRUD for banners/tasks/campaigns/visits/quick-access. No critical bugs found. Minor code-review findings reported.

minor.issue: referred_by stored as lowercase but no validation that the referrer exists. Invalid referral codes are silently accepted.

min_withdraw check happens after the insufficient-balance check; if user balance < min_withdraw*rate the user sees 'Insufficient balance' instead of the more accurate min-withdraw message. Re-order checks for clearer UX.

When status is set to 'paid' with reduced amount, total_withdrawn is incremented by the paid portion which is correct; however the original amount field is overwritten so audit trail loses the originally requested amount. Consider storing 'original_amount' separately.
Streak rolls over from day 30 → 1 instead of capping. Per typical UX, day cap should hold at 30 once reached or restart explicitly. Confirm with product.
Re-watching adds chances unbounded (no daily cap). Could be exploited to farm rewards. Consider daily max chances per game.

action_items: Optional: validate referred_by username exists during registration so invalid codes return 400.
Optional: cap or document daily chance refills from /games/watch-rewarded.
Optional: preserve original requested amount on partial 'deducted' withdrawals for audit.

Tests test_withdraw_success and test_withdraw_action_rejected_refunds are dependent on accumulated random rewards exceeding 1000 pts. They were skipped this run; not a bug. If deterministic coverage is desired, seed user with sufficient points before the withdraw test.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~1260 lines and mixes models, seeding, routes, admin, and helpers. Recommend splitting into modules (models.py, seed.py, routers/{auth,games,tasks,explore,wallet,admin}.py) once feature set stabilises.
All endpoints correctly exclude MongoDB _id via projection or clean() — good.
Admin auth uses opaque session tokens with no expiry. For production add TTL and revocation.
/api/auth/register stores mobile as raw string and only checks isdigit + len>=10. Indian mobile should be exactly 10 digits and ideally start with 6-9.
seed_data_creation: Test creates one fresh user per session using a random device_id + username via /api/auth/register. Admin CRUD tests prefix data with 'TEST_' and delete it at end of each test. No production data modified.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Reuse /app/backend/tests/test_cashclick_backend.py. Each pytest session creates a new device_id; persistent collections may accumulate test users — acceptable. Two withdraw-flow tests will skip unless the random reward path during quizzes/surveys/visits produces enough points. To guarantee execution, either bump initial points via direct DB insert or add an admin endpoint to credit a user. Frontend tests intentionally skipped per request.
